sources.abstract: transmission of 100G PAM-6 and PAM-8 signals for 10-km intra- data center interconnects (intra-DCI) in C-band, which is enabled by pre-chirping technique utilizing a commercial dual-arm Mach- Zehnder modulator (MZM). At the transmitter side, the use of pre-chirping technology can overcome frequency selective fading (FSF) caused by ﬁber dispersion (CD) in intensity modulation and direct detection (IM/DD) systems. The pre-chirping is realized by adjusting the bias voltage of dual-arm MZM operating at sin- gle driven state. At the receiver, V olterra Non-Linear Equalizer (VNLE) is used to eliminate nonlinear impairments caused by pre-chirping. With the joint pre-chirping and nonlinear elimination in single driven dual-arm MZM based IM/DD system, 45-Gbaud PAM-6 and 40-Gbaud PAM-8 signals are successfully transmitted over 10-km single mode ﬁber (SMF) with a bit-error-ratio (BER) below 3.8 × 10−3. While in the chirp-free IM/DD system with dual-arm MZM operating at push-pull state, BERs of 45-Gbaud PAM-6 and 40-Gbaud PAM-8 signals over 10-km SMF transmis- sion deteriorate dramatically to nearly 1 × 10−1. The experimental results show that our proposed pre-chirping scheme can effectively enhance the robustness of IM/DD systems against the CD-induced FSF, and this scheme is a promising candidate for 10-km 100G intra-DCI. JOURNAL OF LIGHTW A VE TECHNOLOGY , VOL. 38, NO. 13, JUL Y 1, 2020 3445
100G PAM-6 and PAM-8 Signal Transmission
Enabled by Pre-Chirping for 10-km Intra-DCI
Utilizing MZM in C-band
Dongdong Zou ,F a nL i , Zibin Li , Wei Wang, Qi Sui, Zizheng Cao , and Zhaohui Li
Abstract—In this paper, we experimentally demonstrate the
transmission of 100G PAM-6 and PAM-8 signals for 10-km intra-
data center interconnects (intra-DCI) in C-band, which is enabled
by pre-chirping technique utilizing a commercial dual-arm Mach-
Zehnder modulator (MZM). At the transmitter side, the use of
pre-chirping technology can overcome frequency selective fading
(FSF) caused by ﬁber dispersion (CD) in intensity modulation and
direct detection (IM/DD) systems. The pre-chirping is realized by
adjusting the bias voltage of dual-arm MZM operating at sin-
gle driven state. At the receiver, V olterra Non-Linear Equalizer
(VNLE) is used to eliminate nonlinear impairments caused by
pre-chirping. With the joint pre-chirping and nonlinear elimination
in single driven dual-arm MZM based IM/DD system, 45-Gbaud
PAM-6 and 40-Gbaud PAM-8 signals are successfully transmitted
over 10-km single mode ﬁber (SMF) with a bit-error-ratio (BER)
below 3.8 × 10−3. While in the chirp-free IM/DD system with
dual-arm MZM operating at push-pull state, BERs of 45-Gbaud
PAM-6 and 40-Gbaud PAM-8 signals over 10-km SMF transmis-
sion deteriorate dramatically to nearly 1 × 10−1. The experimental
results show that our proposed pre-chirping scheme can effectively
enhance the robustness of IM/DD systems against the CD-induced
FSF, and this scheme is a promising candidate for 10-km 100G
intra-DCI.

sources.abstract: passive optical networks (PON), IEEE 802.3ca has commenced discussion of the ﬁrst 100 Gb/s-based PON standard in the form of 100 G Ethernet PON (100G-EPON), in anticipation of growing bandwidth demand by emerging applications such as ﬁxed-mobile convergence for 5G and beyond 5G (B5G) where mobile front-haul and mobile back-haul (MFH/MBF) will require greatly increased capacity of up to 1 Tb/s. In the move toward these 100 Gb/s to 1 Tb/s class PONs, research into the latest coherent technology based PON systems is being pursued due to its high receiver sensitivity at much higher bit rates, superior to current direct-detection based PON systems, aided by the rapid progress of the latest small-scale dig- ital signal processing (DSP) suitable for access spans of less than a few tens of km. In this paper, we review the recent progress of such 100 Gb/s to 1 Tb/s class coherent PON technology. As the key successful factor for realizing 100 Gb/s to 1 Tb/s based coherent PON systems, the latest trends in coherent DSP and the accom- panying embedded coherent transceivers are summarized from the point of view of miniaturization. In addition, highlighting the strong progress with the upcoming coherent PON systems, the ﬁrst demonstration of a 100 Gb/s/ λ × 8 wavelengths (800 Gb/s) based real-time wavelength division multiplexing (WDM)-PON system using a simpliﬁed DSP suitable for access spans is presented. A 100 Gb/s/λ based time division multiplexing (TDM)-PON system is also introduced, addressing the burst-mode coherent reception technique which will be the main issue for achieving practical TDM-based coherent PON systems. capacity of up to 1 Tb/s. In the move toward these 100 Gb/s to 1 Tb/s
class PONs, research into the latest coherent technology based PON
systems is being pursued due to its high receiver sensitivity at much
higher bit rates, superior to current direct-detection based PON
systems, aided by the rapid progress of the latest small-scale dig-
ital signal processing (DSP) suitable for access spans of less than
a few tens of km. In this paper, we review the recent progress of
such 100 Gb/s to 1 Tb/s class coherent PON technology. As the key
successful factor for realizing 100 Gb/s to 1 Tb/s based coherent
PON systems, the latest trends in coherent DSP and the accom-
panying embedded coherent transceivers are summarized from

sources.abstract: Terahertz bands are central to future 6G networks but stress conventional electronics with stringent bandwidth and linearity demands. At the same time, many emerging applications—autonomous driving, smart cities, and dense IoT, require integrated sensing and communication (ISAC). This work targets these two requirements with a photonics-assisted ISAC architecture that (i) generates wideband multi-channel THz signals efficiently and coherently, (ii) mitigates transmission-induced nonlinearities using AI-based equalization. Specifically, we propose and demonstrate an AI-assisted dual-channel single-sideband (SSB) photonics-assisted THz ISAC system in which a discrete multitone (DMT) waveform serves the communication function and a linear frequency-modulated (LFM) waveform realizes radar ranging. By deriving both channels from a single optical source, our design keeps all sub-channels frequency-and-phase-locked, greatly suppressing phase-noise coupling between sensing and communication paths and enabling stable joint processing. We employ a multi-channel SSB vector THz signal generation scheme implemented with a DAC-driven I/Q modulator composed of two sub-MZMs and a cascaded phase modulator (PM). The complex SSB composite is constructed by combining a data-bearing upper sideband (USB) and a data-free lower sideband (LSB). After electro-optic modulation and photodetection, heterodyning yields two target RF/THz sub-channels. The transmitter thus produces a QPSK-DMT communication signal and an LFM sensing signal that share a single laser, ensuring coherent, phase-locked operation across channels (Fig.2). At the receiving end, the communication branch processed by standard DSP (CMA, FOE, CPR) followed by a deep neural network (DNN) nonlinear equalizer, and the sensing branch processed by microwave-photonics de-chirping to remove frequency chirp and recover a single-tone beat (Fig.4). During our  --- Page 18 --- AOS simulation, we choose a bit rate of 20 Gbps, a sampling rate of 64 GSa/s, and a laser at 193.5 THz. The local oscillator for downconversion is 72 GHz. At the transmitter, the initial LFM center frequency is 80 GHz with 25 GHz bandwidth, the QPSK-DMT center frequency is 120 GHz with 30 GHz bandwidth, and the LSB signal is -50 GHz. After photonic SSB processing and heterodyning, the received center frequencies are shifted to 130 GHz for sensing and 170 GHz for communication (Fig.6). Across simulations, the composite SNR ranges 14–24 dB at the receiver. For the communication branch at the receiving end, if we use only conventional DSP, the BER of QPSK-DMT signal at 24 dB SNR is 1.7×10-3 (Fig.7). After introducing the DNN equalizer, BER decrease to 2.5×10-4 at 24 dB SNR. As SNR degrades, the DSP-only BER rises sharply past the SD-FEC, while the AI-assisted receiver maintains BER < 2×10-2 at 18 dB SNR (Fig.7). These results confirm that our lightweight neural equalizer can effectively improve system performance. For the sensing branch, we observe that the range resolution of the LFM radar in our simulation system is 6 mm, in line with ΔR=c/(2B) for the employed LFM bandwidth (Fig.8). In addition, comparing spectra before and after DNN equalization (Fig.8c) shows that AI primarily reduces nonlinear noise on the communication path. Radar range resolution is governed by LFM bandwidth and phase stability and therefore sees no intrinsic resolution gain from DNN. The crucial enabler for high-fidelity sensing is the single-laser, SSB-based generation that preserves phase coherence across channels, minimizing phase-noise-induced ranging errors. The proposed dual-channel SSB photonic ISAC approach simultaneously delivers 20 Gbps data transmission with BER 2.5×10-4 at 24 dB SNR and 6 mm resolution at 130/170 GHz center frequencies using a single optical source. This co-design of waveform generation (WDM-style multi-channel SSB with coherent sub-channels) and AI-assisted reception addresses three pain points in ISAC: (1) bandwidth/linearity bottlenecks of electronics, (2) phase-noise coupling between sensing and communication, and (3) nonlinear distortion accumulation. AI 辅助双通道单边带光子辅助太赫兹
通信感知一体化系统
欧志航 1，周雯 1,2*，王起航 1，徐思聪 1，张杰 1，葛景涛 1，林静雯 1，王思齐 1，王童 1，马
源 1，张腾升 1
1 复旦大学未来信息创新学院，上海 200433；
2 复旦大学集成电路与系统国家重点实验室, 上海, 200433；
摘 要 光子辅助的太赫兹产生与传输技术能有效解决电信号处理太赫兹信号时的带宽问题。
本研究基于光子辅助的太赫兹产生与传输技术，搭建了一种 AI 辅助的双通道单边带通信感
知一体化系统，该系统通过光子辅助技术，分别使用离散多音调制(Discrete Multitone, DMT)
与线性调频(Linear Frequency Modulation, LFM)技术实现通信与感知功能。在系统传输信号
生成方面，采用多通道单边带矢量太赫兹信号生成技术生成 QPSK-QAM 信号以及 LFM 雷
达测距信号。实验中测试的信噪比(Signal-to-Noise Ratio, SNR)范围为 14~24 dB，感知信号
与通信信号的中心频率分别为 130 GHz 与 170 GHz。仿真实验结果表明采用单个光源作为激
励信号来产生多通道的感知信号与通信信号，它们之间具有锁频锁相的特点，成功避免了相
位噪声对通感一体信号的影响。另外，为了降低信号传输中非线性损伤带来的影响，采用神
经网络均衡消除了非线性噪声。当系统比特率为 20 Gbps，在信道信噪比为 24 dB 的条件下
神经网络非线性均衡后的误码性能从 1.7×10-3降低至 2.5×10-4，与此同时测距分辨率为 6 mm，
与理论计算结果一致，在未来 6G 通感一体化中具有广阔的应用前景。
关键词 通感一体化；太赫兹；光子辅助技术；雷达测距；双通道；神经网络

1 引 言
在消费者的展望中，万物互联和智慧城市是未来的信息世界的重要特征，智能通信将使
通信更加敏捷、迅速、友好和便捷，它正在走进人们的生活，让人们可以自由选择通信方式
和设备，人与人之间的对话将变得更加方便和丰富[1]。在日常的生产与生活中，许多情形
下不仅需要高速的智能通信，同时也需要设备对于周围环境的感知能力，如自动驾驶等，并
且随着车辆自动化水平的提高，对于环境的感知能力的要求同样稳步上升[2]。因此此时需
要将通信与感知两个功能运行在同一系统中，而由于雷达与无线通信在很多方面都表现出极

--- Page 3 ---
AOS
高的相似性，两者在发射机、接收机都需要使用到信号发生器、功率放大器、天线等关键器
件来进行信号的处理[3]。根据 IMT-2030(6G)推进组发布的关于 6G 网络的研究报告,通信感
知一体化技术将成为未来 6G 网络的关键技术之一[4]。
由于太赫兹波段带宽资源丰富，6G 网络将大量使用太赫兹波段来进行通信，这将会导
致用于信息传输的频谱与用于雷达感知的频谱产生部分重叠，因此在太赫兹波段的高性能通
信感知一体化技术的实现迫在眉睫。因此光子辅助的太赫兹信号产生与传输技术将在通信感
知一体化的实现过程中发挥重要作用[5]。
实现通信感知一体化的另一关键是通感信号波形设计，目前在通信感知一体化系统中常
见的的信号模型可分为共享信号模型与复用信号模型。其中共享信号模型是指雷达端与通信
端共用同一波形，通常是以 LFM 信号为载波[6]-[7]并搭载 AM[8]、OOK[9]、ASK[10]、MSK[11]
等通信信息，或是以 OFDM 信号[12]作为信号波形同时进行无线传输与雷达探测，因此通
感一体化技术研究的核心与难点在于共享信号波形的设计和相应信号处理。对此，本实验中
引入了一种多通道单边带矢量太赫兹信号生成方案来生成通感系统的共享发送信号。该方法
使用波分复用(Wavelength Division Multiplexing, WDM)的方式构造共享信号，WDM 方法优
势在于能够进一步提高信道容量[13]-[15]，但需要解决其发射机的成本、复杂性以及稳定性
方面的问题[16]。与此同时，相位噪声也是系统中必须考虑的影响因素。对此我们采用光学
SSB 的信号调制方式，在发射端，通过基于软件的 DSP 生成 I 和 Q 输入的数字多通道驱动
信号，在 SSB 调制中通过下边带(Lower Side Band, LSB)信号与上边带(Upper Side Band, USB)
信号进行外差混频，并且单边带调制采用同一个激光源，这样的调制方式可在不额外增加激
光本振的前提下使多个子通道的矢量太赫兹信号同步生成[17]，并使双子通道的信号保持锁
频锁相的特点，即多路信号的频率和相位严格同步，以确保系统输出信号的稳定性和相干性，
有利于后续通信与感知的解调与去啁啾稳定性，有效降低了相位噪声。
最后，在信号的接收方面，传统的接收端 DSP 主要被用于克服线性损伤，但对非线性
损伤的效果并不明显，而在本次实验的通感系统中非线性损伤大量存在。因此，为了提高接
收端准确率，使用神经网络来缓解非线性损伤是较为有效的，基于人工神经网络(Artificial
Neural Network, ANN)[18]-[19]、深度神经网络(Deep Neural Network, DNN)[20]-[21]、卷积神
经网络(Convolutional Neural Network, CNN)[22]-[23]、长短期记忆(Long Short-Term Memory,
LSTM)网络作为众所周知的递归神经网络(Recurrent Neural Network, RNN)[24]-[25]模型的
不同范式是 DSP 领域广泛应用的技术之一[26]。
目前主流的 ISAC 系统仍是电系统，其带宽、线性度以及硬件复杂度等方面存在一定的

--- Page 4 ---

劣势，因此我们在研究中引入光子辅助系统。与此同时，使用光子辅助的系统难以避免的会
产生非线性噪声。现有的传统信号处理方式难以处理非线性噪声的影响，因此我们搭建了光
子辅助太赫兹系统，通过单边带矢量信号生成技术进行通感信号的生成与传输，最后在接收
端使用非线性神经网络均衡器进行均衡，综合上述技术搭建了本研究中的仿真实验模型。表

仅能够通过提高雷达信号的带宽来提升雷达系统感知分辨率，还可以便捷地在太赫兹波段实
现通信感知一体化。进一步的，使用光子辅助的多通道 SSB 太赫兹信号产生思路允许在同
一光源下扩展出更多的子通道以供通信或其他功能，在未来的研究中具有更强的延展性与更
高的适配性。在本文中，我们搭建了一个太赫兹通信感知一体化系统。仿真实验中测试的信
噪比范围为 14~24 dB，系统比特率为 20 Gbps，感知信号与通信信号的中心频率分别为 130
GHz 与 170 GHz。仿真实验结果表明该系统的测距误差能控制在 6 mm，在使用传统 DSP 方
法时通信误比特率在信道信噪比为 24 dB 的条件下达到 1.7×10-3。但信噪比恶化时通信端
BER 将迅速提高至超过软判决误码门限。使用 AI 辅助的方法，该系统的通信端 BER 在信
噪比为 24 dB 的条件下可提升至达 2.5×10-4，当信噪比恶化至 18 dB 时，BER 仍能保持低于

We consider that the proposed model is a powerful tool for the numerical design of strongly band-limited IMDD systems using receiver equalization, as it happens in most of modern and future M-PAM solutions for short reach and access systems. We develop the model as an extension of a previously presented one, and then we test its accuracy by sweeping the main parameters of a 4-PAM-based communication system, such as RIN coefﬁcient, extinction ratio (ER), accumulated CD, equalizer type and memory. Our ﬁndings show a remarkable agreement between time-domain simulations and analytical results, with SNR discrepancies below 0.1 dB in most cases, for both feed-forward and decision-feedback equalization. Moreover, we tested our model predictions against experimental measurements, conﬁrming its ac- curacy. Abstract—In this article, we propose an analytical model to
estimate the signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and then the Bit Error Rate
(BER) at the output of a receiver adaptive equalizer in intensity
modulation and direct detection (IMDD) optical transmission sys-
tems affected by optoelectronic bandwidth limitations, chromatic
dispersion (CD), quantization noise, relative intensity noise (RIN),
shot noise and thermal noise. We consider that the proposed model
is a powerful tool for the numerical design of strongly band-limited
IMDD systems using receiver equalization, as it happens in most of
modern and future M-PAM solutions for short reach and access
systems. We develop the model as an extension of a previously
presented one, and then we test its accuracy by sweeping the
main parameters of a 4-PAM-based communication system, such as
RIN coefﬁcient, extinction ratio (ER), accumulated CD, equalizer
type and memory. Our ﬁndings show a remarkable agreement
between time-domain simulations and analytical results, with SNR
discrepancies below 0.1 dB in most cases, for both feed-forward
and decision-feedback equalization. Moreover, we tested our model
predictions against experimental measurements, conﬁrming its ac-
curacy.

Despite the powerful equalization abil- ity, the required high computation complexity limits its real-time implementation on the hardware. To simplify the complexity, the structure of the VNLE needs to be well optimized. Traditionally, manual optimization of the VNLE structure is blind, and traver- sal search is inefﬁcient and may result in multiple structures. Therefore, the optimal VNLE structure with the best equaliza- tion performance and the lowest equalization complexity for the same performance are still unclear . In this paper, a novel search method named AutoV olterra is proposed to efﬁciently ﬁnd the optimal structure of VNLE by using deep reinforcement learning to learn the functional relationship between structure parameters and optimization objectives. Experimental demonstration of 50- Gb/s PAM-4 signal transmission in a bandwidth-limited intensity modulation and direct detection system is performed to verify the effectiveness of AutoV olterra. Both AutoV olterra and greedy search are applied to V olterra feedforward equalizer, V olterra decision feedback equalizer and V olterra-Pruning. The bit error ratio and equalizer’s complexity results suggest that the structure searched by AutoV olterra can optimize the equalization performance, and also signiﬁcantly reduce the complexity, and improve the pruning quality of VNLE to assist real-time implementation on the hard- ware. Besides, AutoV olterra can also provide a reliable equalization performance benchmark and complexity benchmark to help us verify the actual capabilities of other algorithms. JOURNAL OF LIGHTW A VE TECHNOLOGY , VOL. 40, NO. 16, AUGUST 15, 2022 5395
Automatic Optimization of V olterra Equalizer With
Deep Reinforcement Learning for
Intensity-Modulated Direct-Detection
Optical Communications
Y ongxin Xu, Luyao Huang , Wenqing Jiang, Lei Xue , Weisheng Hu , and Lilin Yi
Abstract—V olterra nonlinear equalizer (VNLE) is widely investi-
gated for linear and nonlinear distortions compensation in optical
communication systems. Despite the powerful equalization abil-
ity, the required high computation complexity limits its real-time
implementation on the hardware. To simplify the complexity, the
structure of the VNLE needs to be well optimized. Traditionally,
manual optimization of the VNLE structure is blind, and traver-
sal search is inefﬁcient and may result in multiple structures.
Therefore, the optimal VNLE structure with the best equaliza-
tion performance and the lowest equalization complexity for the
same performance are still unclear . In this paper, a novel search
method named AutoV olterra is proposed to efﬁciently ﬁnd the
optimal structure of VNLE by using deep reinforcement learning
to learn the functional relationship between structure parameters
and optimization objectives. Experimental demonstration of 50-
Gb/s PAM-4 signal transmission in a bandwidth-limited intensity
modulation and direct detection system is performed to verify the
effectiveness of AutoV olterra. Both AutoV olterra and greedy search
are applied to V olterra feedforward equalizer, V olterra decision
feedback equalizer and V olterra-Pruning. The bit error ratio and
equalizer’s complexity results suggest that the structure searched
by AutoV olterra can optimize the equalization performance, and
also signiﬁcantly reduce the complexity, and improve the pruning
quality of VNLE to assist real-time implementation on the hard-
ware. Besides, AutoV olterra can also provide a reliable equalization
performance benchmark and complexity benchmark to help us
verify the actual capabilities of other algorithms.
Index Terms —Deep reinforcement learning, direct detection,
intensity modulation, structure optimization, V olterra equalizer .

of users. To support high-speed transmission in optical data
center interconnections and access networks, while meeting
the strict requirements for low-cost and energy-efﬁciency, the
simple intensity-modulated direct-detection (IM/DD) systems
using only one single photodiode (PD) for signal reception
are considered as the most cost-effective option [1]–[4]. Due
to linear distortions such as limited system bandwidth, ﬁber
chromatic dispersion (CD), and nonlinear distortions caused
by PD’s square-law detection and modulator nonlinearity, the
performance of IM/DD system is limited when the transmission
speed reach up to 50 Gb/s. To overcome these impairments,
digital nonlinear equalization techniques, especially V olterra
nonlinear equalizers (VNLEs) based on the V olterra series [5]–
[10], including V olterra feedforward equalizer (V olterra-FFE)
and V olterra decision feedback equalizer (V olterra-DFE), have
drawn widely attention due to their capability to compensate
for both linear and nonlinear distortions. Nonetheless, they tend
to require a high computational complexity which prohibits
its real-time implementation in the cost-sensitive short reach
ﬁled. How to reduce the complexity of VNLE while keep the
equalization performance is the research focus.

be divided into two categories. The ﬁrst one is to obtain a sim-

to other systems. The other one is the pruning technique by
removing unimportant taps of the equalizer, including unstruc-

sources.title: Capacity Limits of Optical Fiber Networks
sources.abstract: of ﬁber-optic communication systems (or “ﬁber channels”) based on information theory. This paper is divided into two parts. Part 1 reviews fundamental concepts of digital communications and information theory. We treat digitization and modulation fol- lowed by information theory for channels both without and with memory. We provide explicit relationships between the commonly used signal-to-noise ratio and the optical signal-to-noise ratio. We further evaluate the performance of modulation constellations such as quadrature-amplitude modulation, combinations of am- plitude-shift keying and phase-shift keying, exotic constellations, and concentric rings for an additive white Gaussian noise channel using coherent detection. Part 2 is devoted speciﬁcally to the ‘‘ﬁber channel.’’ We review the physical phenomena present in trans- mission over optical ﬁber networks, including sources of noise, the need for optical ﬁltering in optically-routed networks, and, most critically, the presence of ﬁber Kerr nonlinearity. We describe various transmission scenarios and impairment mitigation tech- niques, and deﬁne a ﬁber channel deemed to be the most relevant for communication over optically-routed networks. We proceed to evaluate a capacity limit estimate for this ﬁber channel using ring constellations. Several scenarios are considered, including uniform and optimized ring constellations, different ﬁber dispersion maps, and varying transmission distances. We further present evidences that point to the physical origin of the ﬁber capacity limitations and provide a comparison of recent record experiments with our capacity limit estimation. Index Terms—, Ampliﬁed spontaneous emission, Brillouin scat- tering, channel coding, detection, ﬁber nonlinearity, information rates, information theory, modulation, noise, optical networks, Raman scattering. Capacity Limits of Optical Fiber Networks
René-Jean Essiambre, Senior Member , IEEE, Fellow, OSA , Gerhard Kramer , Fellow, IEEE ,
Peter J. Winzer, Fellow, IEEE, Gerard J. Foschini , Fellow, IEEE, and Bernhard Goebel , Student Member , IEEE
(Invited Paper)
Abstract—We describe a method to estimate the capacity limit
of ﬁber-optic communication systems (or “ﬁber channels”) based
on information theory. This paper is divided into two parts. Part
1 reviews fundamental concepts of digital communications and
information theory. We treat digitization and modulation fol-
lowed by information theory for channels both without and with
memory. We provide explicit relationships between the commonly
used signal-to-noise ratio and the optical signal-to-noise ratio. We
further evaluate the performance of modulation constellations
such as quadrature-amplitude modulation, combinations of am-
plitude-shift keying and phase-shift keying, exotic constellations,
and concentric rings for an additive white Gaussian noise channel
using coherent detection. Part 2 is devoted speciﬁcally to the ‘‘ﬁber
channel.’’ We review the physical phenomena present in trans-
mission over optical ﬁber networks, including sources of noise, the
need for optical ﬁltering in optically-routed networks, and, most
critically, the presence of ﬁber Kerr nonlinearity. We describe
various transmission scenarios and impairment mitigation tech-
niques, and deﬁne a ﬁber channel deemed to be the most relevant
for communication over optically-routed networks. We proceed to
evaluate a capacity limit estimate for this ﬁber channel using ring
constellations. Several scenarios are considered, including uniform
and optimized ring constellations, different ﬁber dispersion maps,
and varying transmission distances. We further present evidences
that point to the physical origin of the ﬁber capacity limitations
and provide a comparison of recent record experiments with our
capacity limit estimation.
Index Terms—, Ampliﬁed spontaneous emission, Brillouin scat-
tering, channel coding, detection, ﬁber nonlinearity, information
rates, information theory, modulation, noise, optical networks,
Raman scattering.

A 10 Gb/s optical duobinary signal can be transmitted over 200 km of standard single-mode ﬁber (SMF) with 1 dB dispersion penalty. The advantages of the proposed system are conﬁrmed experimentally. As the transmission distance without regenerative repeaters is extended, ﬁber chromatic dispersion becomes an important factor limiting the transmission distance. 1530 JOURNAL OF LIGHTWA VE TECHNOLOGY, VOL. 15, NO. 8, AUGUST 1997
Dispersion-Tolerant Optical Transmission System
Using Duobinary Transmitter
and Binary Receiver
Kazushige Yonenaga and Shigeru Kuwano
Abstract—We propose a dispersion-tolerant optical duobinary
transmission system which uses a binary intensity modulation
direct detection (IM–DD) receiver. The proposed system also
relaxes the ﬁber input power limitation due to stimulated Bril-
louin scattering (SBS). Tolerance to chromatic dispersion in the
proposed system is estimated by a simulation. A 10 Gb/s optical
duobinary signal can be transmitted over 200 km of standard
single-mode ﬁber (SMF) with 1 dB dispersion penalty. The
advantages of the proposed system are conﬁrmed experimentally.
The optical duobinary signal was successfully received by the
binary IM–DD receiver at the same sensitivity as a binary IM
signal. Power penalty due to chromatic dispersion over a 164-km
standard SMF was about 1.8 dB at 10 Gb/s. The SBS threshold
of the 10 Gb/s optical duobinary modulated light was more than
+20 dBm.
Index Terms— Chromatic dispersion, duobinary code, optical
transmission, stimulated Brillouin scattering.
I. I NTRODUCTION
T
HE advent of erbium-doped ﬁber ampliﬁers (EDFA’s)
operating in the 1.55 m region has extended the trans-
mission distance as limited by ﬁber loss in optical ﬁber
transmission systems. As the transmission distance without
regenerative repeaters is extended, ﬁber chromatic dispersion
becomes an important factor limiting the transmission distance.
The chromatic dispersion causes severe intersymbol interfer-
ence in high-speed optical transmission systems. It has been
shown by simulation analysis that a 1-dB dispersion penalty
is caused after only 65 km standard single-mode ﬁber (SMF)
transmission in a 10-Gb/s intensity modulation direct detection
(IM–DD) system [1].
One approach to reduce the chromatic dispersion effect is
to insert a dispersive optical component in the optical ﬁber
link. Several optical dispersion compensation devices have
been reported [2]–[5]. They are effective in compensating
the chromatic dispersion, but inserting the additional optical
components increases optical loss and additional optical ampli-
ﬁcation may be required. Moreover, it is necessary to carefully
adjust the magnitude of the dispersion of optical components
according to the ﬁber chromatic dispersion.
Several techniques for extending the dispersion limit with-
out optical dispersion compensation devices have been re-

ported. One is the prechirp technique [6]. This technique
utilizes the pulse compression effect due to the frequency
chirping of an intensity modulated (IM) signal; the frequency
chirping is controlled according to the chromatic dispersion
of the transmission ﬁber. Another is the dispersion-supported
transmission technique [7]. This technique utilizes FM (fre-
quency modulation)–AM (amplitude modulation) conversion
in optical ﬁber. When an optical frequency-shift keying (FSK)
signal is transmitted, the AM component generated within the
transmission ﬁber is detected by a direct detection receiver. In
this technique, frequency deviation of the optical FSK signal
must be adjusted according to the chromatic dispersion of the
transmission ﬁber so that the group delay difference between
the two frequency components corresponding to binary data
“0” and “1” equals one bit duration.
Use of bandlimiting codes also makes it possible to extend
the dispersion limit without additional optical components
[8]–[10]. This technique utilizes the fact that a narrow band
signal has higher tolerance to chromatic dispersion than a
broad band signal. As this technique is not a dispersion
compensation technique and does not utilize the chromatic
dispersion itself, it is not necessary to adjust any parameters
according to the chromatic dispersion of the transmission ﬁber.
It was recently reported that optical three-level transmission
using a duobinary code could overcome the transmission
distance limitation due to ﬁber chromatic dispersion in a 10-
Gb/s transmission system [9], [10]. It is well known that the
duobinary code can be used to reduce the signal bandwidth for
the same transmission rate. Such a transmission system needs
electrical duobinary decoding. Therefore, additional circuits
are required at the receiver. Moreover, the three-level decision
causes some degradation in receiver sensitivity compared with
a binary IM–DD transmission system.
In this paper, we propose an optical duobinary transmis-
sion system utilizing optical phase, which has the following
advantages. 1) A binary IM–DD receiver can be used and no
receiver sensitivity degradation is caused. 2) Higher tolerance
to ﬁber chromatic dispersion than is possible with a binary

sources.title: End-to-End Learning for 100G-PON Based on Noise Adaptation Network
sources.abstract: ize the optimal performance of optical communication systems. By replacing the independent signal processing modules in the transmitter and receiver with deep neural networks, end-to-end system optimization can be achieved via training the neural net- works together on a differentiable channel. In this paper, a noise adaptation network for channel modeling is proposed to simulate channel response and the impact of channel noise on transmitted signals. The structure of the noise adaptation network is multi-scale deep neural network (MscaleDNN), which can better characterize the channel at different frequencies. Based on the noise adapta- tion network, a novel end-to-end learning framework is further designed. Within the framework, memory buffer technology and constraint loss are introduced to signiﬁcantly enhance the efﬁciency and performance of end-to-end learning. Experimental demonstra- tion of the proposed end-to-end learning scheme is performed on a 100G passive optical network (PON) system based on intensity modulation and direct detection. The results indicate that, com- pared to the optimized V olterra non-linear equalization at the receiver and the joint equalization achieved by indirect approach, end-to-end optimization improves receiver sensitivity by 0.8 dB and 1.2 dB, respectively, and achieves a power budget of 31.4 dB. In particular, the advantages of the end-to-end learning are even more pronounced in the case of higher received optical power . Index Terms —Constraint loss, end-to-end learning, memory buffer, MscaleDNN, noise adaptation network, PON. I. End-to-End Learning for 100G-PON Based on
Noise Adaptation Network
Y ongxin Xu , Luyao Huang , Wenqing Jiang , Xiaokai Guan , Weisheng Hu , and Lilin Yi
Abstract—End-to-end learning is a promising solution to real-
ize the optimal performance of optical communication systems.
By replacing the independent signal processing modules in the
transmitter and receiver with deep neural networks, end-to-end
system optimization can be achieved via training the neural net-
works together on a differentiable channel. In this paper, a noise
adaptation network for channel modeling is proposed to simulate
channel response and the impact of channel noise on transmitted
signals. The structure of the noise adaptation network is multi-scale
deep neural network (MscaleDNN), which can better characterize
the channel at different frequencies. Based on the noise adapta-
tion network, a novel end-to-end learning framework is further
designed. Within the framework, memory buffer technology and
constraint loss are introduced to signiﬁcantly enhance the efﬁciency
and performance of end-to-end learning. Experimental demonstra-
tion of the proposed end-to-end learning scheme is performed on
a 100G passive optical network (PON) system based on intensity
modulation and direct detection. The results indicate that, com-
pared to the optimized V olterra non-linear equalization at the
receiver and the joint equalization achieved by indirect approach,
end-to-end optimization improves receiver sensitivity by 0.8 dB and
1.2 dB, respectively, and achieves a power budget of 31.4 dB. In
particular, the advantages of the end-to-end learning are even more
pronounced in the case of higher received optical power .
Index Terms —Constraint loss, end-to-end learning, memory
buffer, MscaleDNN, noise adaptation network, PON.

Abstract—In intensity-modulation and direct-detection (IMDD)
single-mode ﬁbre (SMF) transmission systems, the nonlinear op-
eration of square-law detection causes signal-signal beating in-
terference (SSBI) that considerably limits the signal transmission
capacity versus reach performance of the systems. To address this
challenge, an error-controlled iterative algorithm (ECIA) with ex-
tra decision thresholds is proposed to digitally linearize the IMDD
transmission systems. In the ECIA, instead of amplitude errors
used in previously reported algorithms, theQ− 2 factor is utilized as
an objective function, along with the proposed stochastic gradient
descent (SGD) optimization of the thresholds for symbol decision.
The thresholds can be adaptively adjusted to reduce decision errors
during iterations. To improve the algorithm’s adaptivity to various
system operation conditions, new constraints are also introduced
including maximum step size and/or sign of threshold variation.
By making use of the identiﬁed optimum key parameters of the
ECIA, numerical investigations are conducted of the effectiveness
of the proposed scheme in supporting 100 Gb/s 4-level pulse am-
plitude modulation (PAM4) transmissions over 80 km standard
SMFs. Results show that compared with the previously reported
decision-directed data-aided iterative algorithm (DD-DIA), the
ECIA signiﬁcantly reduces the power/optical signal-to-noise ratio
(OSNR) penalty, required minimum number of iterations (con-
vergence rate) and improves the wavelength drift tolerance. For
the ECIA, an OSNR penalty of ≤ 3.8 dB (10% overhead) and a
minimum iteration count of 50 are observed for up to 400 km SMF
transmissions, showing its robustness to accumulated dispersions of
long ﬁbres. More importantly, a pilot-free operation (0% overhead)
is also feasible at a cost of a slightly increased OSNR penalty of
≤ 0.5 dB.

Abstract—In this article, we discuss challenges and options
for scaling IM/DD transceivers towards 800 Gbps. Our focus is
CWDM4 PAM4 transmission and our target distance is 10 km in
O-band, which is a most urgent use case for next generation optical
short reach systems like data centre interconnects and networks.
At this reach and rate, chromatic dispersion (CD) becomes the
main challenge. Its mitigation is essential and primarily done with
digital signal processing. State of the art techniques, however, make

performance in both cases. In any of these conﬁgurations, a key
enabler against strong CD penalties is duobinary training, which

10 km to 6 km [5]. In this article, 200 Gb/s per lane is realized
via a line rate of 112GBd PAM4, accounting for typical forward
error correction (FEC) overheads.

sources.title: High Dynamic Range 100G PON Enabled by SOA Preamplifier and Recurrent Neural Networks
sources.abstract: focused on future systems targeting 100 Gb/s/ λ and beyond, with digital signal processing seen as a key enabling technology. Spec- trally efﬁcient 4-level pulse amplitude modulation (PAM4) is seen as a cost-effective solution that exploits the ready availability of cheaper, low-bandwidth devices, and Semiconductor Optical Am- pliﬁers (SOA) are being investigated as receiver preampliﬁers to compensate PAM4’s high signal-to-noise ratio requirements and meet the demanding 29 dB PON loss budget. However, SOA gain saturation-induced patterning distortion is a concern in the context of PON burst-mode signalling, and the 19.5 dB loud-soft packet dynamic range expected by the most recent ITU-T 50G standards. In this article we propose a recurrent neural network equalisation technique based on gated recurrent units (GRU-RNN) to not only mitigate SOA patterning affecting loud packet bursts, but to also exploit their remarkable effectiveness at compensating non-linear impairments to unlock the SOA gain saturated regime. Using such an equaliser we demonstrate > 28 dB system dynamic range in 100 Gb/s PAM4 system by using SOA gain compression in conjunction with GRU-RNN equalisation. We ﬁnd that our pro- posed GRU-RNN has similar equalisation capabilities as non-linear V olterra, fully connected neural network, and long short-term memory based equalisers, but observe that feedback-based RNN equalisers are more suited to the varying levels of impairment inherent to PON burst-mode signalling due to their low input tap requirements. Recognising issues surrounding hardware im- plementation of RNNs, we investigate a multi-symbol equalisation scheme to lower the feedback latency requirements of our proposed GRU-RNN. Finally, we compare equaliser complexities and perfor- mances according to trainable parameters and real valued multipli- cation operations, ﬁnding that the proposed GRU-RNN equaliser is more efﬁcient than those based on V olterra, fully connected neural networks or long short-term memory units proposed elsewhere. Index Terms —Digital signal processing, four-level pulse amplitude modulation, machine learning, optical ﬁber communications, passive optical network, semiconductor optical ampliﬁer . I. Di Che, Qian Hu, and William Shieh, Fellow, IEEE

Abstract—Intensity modulation with direct detection (IM-DD)
dominates the commercial short-reach optical communications.
However, when upgrading the data-rate distance product to
1000 Gb/s·km per wavelength and beyond, IM-DD faces severe
performance barrier. Although a linear mapping exists between
the intensity of transmitter and receiver in IM-DD back-to-back
system, it becomes nonlinear under ﬁber channel impairments;
for instance, the dominant chromatic dispersion. Coherent detec-
tion overcomes this fundamental obstacle by recovering a linear
replica of the optical ﬁeld instead of intensity. The local oscillator
provides a reference carrier which mixes with the received signal,
from which both intensity and phase of the signal can be recovered.
Borrowing the idea from coherent detection, in DD system, a car-
rier can be sent along with the signal so that the receiver utilizes
the reference carrier originated from transmitter; namely, the re-
ceiver conducts self-coherent (SCOH) detection. In this paper, we
review a variety of optical SCOH subsystems, and reveal how they
realize linear channels, while maintaining the simple and low-cost
DD. SCOH can readily reach a data-rate distance product beyond
10000 Gb/s·km, making it suitable for the future high-speed short-
and medium-reach applications, such as the data center intercon-
nect, passive access network, and metropolitan area network.
Index Terms—Coherent detection, direct detection, frequency
selective fading channels, linearization techniques, modulation,
optical ﬁber communication.

The revival of coherent optical systems has successfully

the LH-ON to a capacity leap beyond multi-Terabit/s.

even 100 Gb/s to meet the ever-increasing trafﬁc demand. Con-

dominated by the IM-DD, it faces the similar bottleneck which
has emerged decades before on the LH-ON—the channel non-
linearity. Nevertheless, it is not practical to directly upgrade the
SMR-ON to coherent systems, due to its cost-sensitive nature.

Index Terms —Digital signal processing, forward error correction, optical ﬁber communication, optical ﬁber networks, optical interconnections. I. I NTRODUCTION T HE use of digital signal processing (DSP) in optical links has about a 20-year history [1]–[4]. To use the powerful DSP techniques, the analog optical signal had to be digitized ﬁrst. This required a low power, high-speed ADC (analog to digital converter) at the receiver. This, together with the devel- opment of a high-speed DAC (digital to analog converter) at the transmitter for signal conditioning, enabled the growth of the modern-day high-speed optical interconnects. The ﬁrst use of DSP was in MLSE (maximum likelihood sequence estimation) implementation for chromatic dispersion compensation in intensity modulated direct detect (IMDD) 10 Gbit/s systems [2], [3]. This was not very power efﬁcient and provided only limited mitigation of chromatic dispersion for long-haul applications; hence MLSE was not deployed widely in 10 Gbit/s IMDD systems. The development of high-speed coherent receivers enabled the linear detection of both signal amplitude and phase. The use of DSP in coherent applications to compensate for a variety of linear optical impairments, as well as enabling QAM (Quadrature Amplitude Modulation), was a very powerful innovation which led to the widespread deployment of DSP hardware in optical links [1], [3]. In 2015, Inphi was the ﬁrst to develop and commercialize a PAM4 (4 level Pulse Amplitude Modulation) DSP for direct detect (DD), 100G, intra data center (DC) applications [5], [6]. Abstract—In this tutorial, we discuss the evolution of the tech-
nology deployed for optical interconnects and the trade-offs in the
design of low complexity, low power DSP and implementation for
direct detect and coherent, pluggable optical modules for data

modulation format, baud rate, optical link design, forward error
correction, signal shaping and dispersion compensation.

Fig. 1 shows a bifurcated technology and product space for
the 100G data center interconnect deployment. Intra (inside)

Return to Zero) format, based on analog CDR (Clock and Data

were a combination of IMDD PAM4 pluggable modules, which

Specialized hardware [11] and lower complexity coherent

conﬁgurations.
Metro (metropolitan) and long-haul designations are used for
backbone connections between large geographic distances.

DC switches from 25G NRZ to 50/100G PAM4 (25/50 Gbaud)
[12].

Intensity modulation and direct detection (IMDD) tech- nology is still considered as a promising candidate for 100 Gb/s/ λ PON attributed to its low cost, low power consumption, and small footprint. In this paper, we achieve 100 Gb/s/ λ IMDD PON by us- ing 20G-class optical and electrical devices due to its commercial availability. To mitigate the system linear and nonlinear distortions, neural network (NN) based equalizer is used and the performance is compared with feedforward equalizer (FFE) and V olterra non- linear equalizer (VNE). We introduce the rules to train and test the data while using NN-based equalizer to guarantee a fair com- parison with FFE and VNE. Random data have to be used for training, but for test, both random data and pseudorandom bit sequence are applicable. We found that the NN-based equalizer has the same performance with FFE and VNE in the case of linear distortion, but outperforms them in a strong nonlinearity case. In the experiment, to improve the loss budget, we increase the launch power to 18 dBm, achieving a 30-dB loss budget for 33 GBd/s PAM8 signal at the system frequency response of 16.2 GHz, attributed to the strong nonlinear equalization capability of NN. sources.title: Modulation Format and Digital Signal Processing for IM-DD Optics at Post-200G Era
sources.abstract: detection (DD) optics are being commercialized to support 800G and 1.6T applications inside datacenters. Though IM-DD remains its cost and power consumption advantages over Coherent at 1.6T for short-reach interconnect below 10 km, its roadmap is not clear towards the next capacity doubling, considering it becomes more challenging to scale the components bandwidth linearly with the capacity demand. This makes both advanced modulation formats and digital signal processing (DSP) indispensable for an IM-DD system aiming at higher speed. From a system perspective, this article reviews candidate modulation formats and DSP for IM-DD optics at post 200G (per lane) era. By taking into account generic constraints for future IM-DD systems like bandwidth limit, peak power constraint, transceiver nonlinearity, ﬁber dispersion, and so on, it discusses a wide range of techniques including probabilistic constellation shaping (PCS), high symbol rate pulse amplitude modulation (PAM), faster than Nyquist (FTN) signaling, nonlinear equalizations, and multicarrier modulations. Different from prior IM-DD review literature, we mainly focus on if it is meaningful to exploit a DSP technique with respect to constraints in practical systems, rather than just the technique itself. The study is backed with rich simulation and experiment results. Index Terms—Digital signal processing (DSP), direct detection (DD), faster than nyquist (FTN), intensity modulation (IM), modulation format, multicarrier modulation, nonlinear equalization, optical ﬁber communication, probabilistic constellation shaping (PCS), pulse amplitude modulation (PAM). Modulation Format and Digital Signal Processing for
IM-DD Optics at Post-200G Era
Di Che and Xi Chen
(Invited Paper)
Abstract—200-Gb/s per lane intensity-modulation (IM) direct-
detection (DD) optics are being commercialized to support 800G
and 1.6T applications inside datacenters. Though IM-DD remains
its cost and power consumption advantages over Coherent at 1.6T
for short-reach interconnect below 10 km, its roadmap is not clear
towards the next capacity doubling, considering it becomes more
challenging to scale the components bandwidth linearly with the
capacity demand. This makes both advanced modulation formats
and digital signal processing (DSP) indispensable for an IM-DD
system aiming at higher speed. From a system perspective, this
article reviews candidate modulation formats and DSP for IM-DD
optics at post 200G (per lane) era. By taking into account generic
constraints for future IM-DD systems like bandwidth limit, peak
power constraint, transceiver nonlinearity, ﬁber dispersion, and so
on, it discusses a wide range of techniques including probabilistic
constellation shaping (PCS), high symbol rate pulse amplitude
modulation (PAM), faster than Nyquist (FTN) signaling, nonlinear
equalizations, and multicarrier modulations. Different from prior
IM-DD review literature, we mainly focus on if it is meaningful
to exploit a DSP technique with respect to constraints in practical
systems, rather than just the technique itself. The study is backed
with rich simulation and experiment results.
Index Terms—Digital signal processing (DSP), direct detection
(DD), faster than nyquist (FTN), intensity modulation (IM),
modulation format, multicarrier modulation, nonlinear
equalization, optical ﬁber communication, probabilistic
constellation shaping (PCS), pulse amplitude modulation (PAM).
I. I NTRODUCTION
D
IGITAL signal processing (DSP) has been applied to
optical ﬁber communications for almost two decades.
Though the ﬁrst use of DSP was in 10-Gb/s intensity modu-
lation (IM) and direct detection (DD) systems to combat the
ﬁber dispersion by maximum likelihood sequence estimation
(MLSE) [1], [2], it soon became a powerful tool to revive
the coherent communication [3], [4]. Nowadays, DSP-enabled
coherent transceivers have been widely implemented to optical
systems ranging from long-haul transmission over thousands
of kilometers (km) to short-reach interconnects below 80 km.
On the other hand, despite a variety of DSP techniques being

with a low-complexity receiver DSP including clock recovery,
equalization like a feed-forward equalizer (FFE) and low-latency
forward error correction (FEC) decoding like the KP4/KR4 FEC
deﬁned in IEEE 802.3 Standard.
Based on 50- and 100-Gb/s per lane IM-DD, optical modules
for 400G Ethernet have been shipping in volume since 2020. In
the meantime, the 200-Gb/s per lane optical standard is being
actively developed in both industry interoperability groups and
standard bodies. Such 100-GBd class PAM-4 signals inevitably
call for more sophisticated DSP to combat system impairments.
For example, the 800G Pluggable MSA group suggests an extra
MLSE after the FFE to improve the tolerance to dispersion and
bandwidth limit, and a stronger concatenated FEC scheme than
KP4 to improve the bit-error ratio (BER) threshold from 2e-4
to 2e-3 [6]. Going beyond 200G per lane, it is perceived DSP
would play an even more important role for an IM-DD system,
considering its component bandwidth growth has begun lagging
the next capacity doubling of optical interface.
In European Conference on Optical Communications (ECOC
2022), we discussed whether it is meaningful to pursue higher
symbol rate in a bandwidth-limited IM-DD system via DSP
[7]. This article extends our ECOC presentation and provides
a more comprehensive review on the potential DSP suitable for
future IM-DD optics. There have been extensive literatures to

JOURNAL OF LIGHTW A VE TECHNOLOGY , VOL. 38, NO. 3, FEBRUARY 1, 2020 583
Optics-Simpliﬁed DSP for 50 Gb/s PON Downstream
Transmission using 10 Gb/s Optical Devices
Lei Xue , Lilin Yi , Weisheng Hu , Rui Lin , and Jiajia Chen
Abstract—Directly-modulated laser (DML) is widely employed
in intensity modulation and direct detection (IMDD) system due
to its low cost and high output power . However, the corresponding
frequency chirp is regarded as one of the main disadvantages for
its application in passive optical networks (PONs). In this paper,
we theoretically analyze the frequency response evolution of DML
based system under different chirp and dispersion conditions,
proving that the system bandwidth can be improved by interactions
between negative dispersion and DML chirp. Based on this concept,
we experimentally demonstrated downstream 50 Gb/s PAM4 signal
transmission over 20 km single-mode ﬁber (SMF) access based on
the 10 Gb/s DML operating at 1310 nm and avalanche photodiode
(APD). A dispersion-shifted ﬁber (DSF) providing −150 ps/nm
dispersion at 1310 nm in the optical line terminal (OLT) is used to
pre-equalize the frequency response of bandwidth-limited directly
modulated signals in the optical domain. Thanks to our proposed
dispersion-supported equalization (DSE) technique, the system
bandwidth can be improved by 5 GHz. Feed-forward equalization
(FFE), decision feedback equalization (DFE) and V olterra ﬁlter
are employed to evaluate the signal performance improvement,
respectively. By evaluating the receiver sensitivity, the DSE com-
bined with FFE scheme shows 2 dB improvement than the complex
V olterra algorithm, indicating its potential to reduce the complexity
of digital signal processing (DSP) and therefore a lower cost and
power consumption in PON.
Index Terms —Digital signal processing (DSP), direct
detection, directly modulated laser (DML), dispersion-supported
equalization (DSE), passive optical network (PON).

Abstract—To address the wavelength collision between 50G 
PON and EPON, this paper discusses the power budget penalty 
when changing the downstream wavelength of 50G PON with 
varying EML chirp factors and DSP combinations. 
Keywords—50G PON, coexistence, EPON, DSP, chirp factor 
I. INTRODUCTION  
In order to meet the bandwidth requirements of residential, 
commercial and wireless broadband services, ITU-T 50G 
Time division multiplexing passive optical network (TDM-
PON) is proposed and published in G.9804 [1-3]. 50G PON 
targets 50 Gb/s downstream (DS) and up to 50 Gb/s upstream 
(US) using a single wavelength in each direction, with Non-
Return-to-Zero On-Off Keying (NRZ-OOK). The electro-
absorption-modulated laser (EML) augmented with a 
semiconductor optical amplifier (SOA) is adopted to ensure 
Transmitter and Dispersion Eye Closure (TDEC) and launch 
power. In order to avoid the power budget penalty caused by 
chromatic dispersion (CD), 1342±2 nm is adopted for DS 
operation [4]. In the process of commercialization, 
coexistence, i.e., the reuse of existing optical distribution 
network (ODN) and compatibility with legacy PON, must be 
considered [5,6]. However, as shown in Fig. 1 (a). the 
wavelength of the 50G PON DS conflicts with that of EPON 
[7]. To address this issue, the wavelength of DS needs to be 
re-selected. On the one hand, the wavelength needs to avoid 
the “water peak” because the op tical fiber deployed in some 
ODNs does not eliminate the “wat er peak”. In addition, the 
optional wavelength is in a longer wavelength area, so the

sources.abstract: Objective  The rapid evolution of 5G and 6G networks demands ultra -large capacity and low latency to  support emerging applications like VR/AR and the Internet of Everything. High -order  quadrature amplitude modulation (QAM) is crucial for increasing spectral efficienc y, but it  typically requires expensive, high -power, and high -precision digital -to-analog converters  (DACs). Delta -Sigma modulation (DSM) offers a solution by using low -resolution  quantization with oversampling, thus simplifying transmitter hardware. However, transmitting  ultra-high-order formats like 16384-QAM using DSM over standard single-mode fiber (SMF)  faces severe challenges. The chromatic dispersion of SMF causes high-frequency quantization  noise to fold back into the signal band, degrading performance. Additionally, the limited signal- to-noise ratio (SNR) of traditional 1-bit DSM is insufficient for 16384-QAM, while increasing  optical power is restricted by fiber nonlinearity. In present study, we report a high -fidelity  mobile fronthaul transmission architecture. This method integrates 2 -bit DSM quantization  with a nested anti -resonant nodeless fiber (NANF) link. The system utilizes the low  nonlinearity and near -zero dispersion of NANF to overcome traditional transmission limits.  We hope that our prop osed scheme can provide a reference for the low -cost generation and  robust transmission of ultra-high-order QAM signals in future mobile fronthaul networks.  Methods  A combined approach of algorithm optimization and system experiment is employed in present  study. First, a 2-bit (4-level) DSM quantization strategy is adopted to break the SNR bottleneck  --- Page 15 --- CJL of traditional 1-bit schemes. By increasing the quantization level, the quantization noise power  is reduced by approximately 6 dB. An 8th -order Noise Transfer Function (NTF) is designed  and optimized. Zeros are placed on the unit circle within the signal b andwidth to form a deep  spectral notch, while poles are constrained within the unit circle to ensure loop stability. Then,  the experimental transmission system is established. At the transmitter, a broadband orthogonal  frequency-division multiplexing (OFDM ) signal carrying high -order QAM symbols is  generated. This signal is up -sampled and quantized into a 105 -Gbaud PAM4 signal by the 2 - bit DSM. The digital signal is converted to an analog waveform by an arbitrary waveform  generator (AWG) and modulated onto an optical carrier via a Mach-Zehnder modulator (MZM).  The optical signal is amplified to 10 dBm and transmitted through a 1.5 -km NANF. This  specific fiber provides a low attenuation of 1 dB/km and negligible dispersion at 1550 nm. At  the receiver, the sig nal is detected by a photodiode (PD) and captured by a real -time  oscilloscope (OSC). Finally, offline digital signal processing (DSP) is performed. This includes  synchronization, channel equalization, and low-pass filtering to remove out-of-band noise and  recover the original high-order QAM information.  Results and Discussions  The spectral characteristics of the signal were analyzed to verify the noise shaping effect. A  distinct noise notch is observed in the received power spectrum (Fig. 5). This indicates that the  quantization noise is effectively pushed to the high -frequency region. At 110 Gbaud, the  spectrum structure of the DSM-OOK signal remains intact without obvious distortion.  The bit error rate (BER) performance was tested under different conditions. For the 1-bit DSM  system, the BER remains flat and below the SD -FEC threshold (4.2×10-2) when the baud rate  is below 105 Gbaud. At 110 Gbaud, the BER begins to rise due to bandwidth limitations. The  BER curves for NANF transmission and back-to-back (BTB) transmission almost overlap (Fig.  6). This indicates that the impairment introduced by t he fiber link is negligible.  Crucially, the experiment demonstrated the transmission of 105 GBaud signals carrying 16384- QAM information using 2-bit DSM. 摘 要 为了解决传输超高阶正交幅度调制（QAM）信号时的非线性损伤及 DAC 精度受限
问题，提出了一种基于 Delta-Sigma 调制（DSM）的嵌套反谐振无节点光纤（NANF）传输
系统。通过使用 2-bit DSM 量化技术，将基带超高阶 QAM 符号序列编码为四电平脉冲幅度
调制（PAM4）信号流。得益于 NANF 与 DSM 结合后优异的传输特性，105 GBaud DSM -
PAM4 信号在 1.5 km 链路中实现了高质量传输。实验结果表明 105 GBaud DSM-PAM4 信号
误码率满足软判决前向纠错（ SD-FEC）阈值（4.2× 10-2）。通过低通滤波去除量化噪声后，
系统成功恢复并解调出 16384-QAM 信号。实验演示了基于 Delta-Sigma 调制的 16384 QAM
信号使用 NANF 传输系统，为超高阶 QAM 信号在低成本硬件下的生成与传输提供了解决
方案，同时也为未来 6G 移动前传提供了参考。 
关键词 嵌套反谐振无节点光纤；Delta-Sigma 调制；16384-QAM；2-bit 量化；6G 前传 
中图分类号 TN91    文献标志码 A 
 
1 引  言 
随着移动互联网、物联网以及增强现实/虚拟现实（AR/VR）等新兴应用的爆发式增
长，全球网络流量呈现出指数级上升趋势。为了满足未来  5G/6G 网络对超大容量、泛在
连接以及确定性低时延的严苛需求，光传输网络作为通信基础设施的基石，正面临着前所
未有的挑战[1]。然而，作为当前光网络基础的实芯单模光纤（SMF） ，由于受到固有的克尔
（Kerr）非线性效应限制，其传输容量正逐渐逼近香农极限 [2]。为了突破这一物理瓶颈，
空芯光纤（HCF）技术应运而生。特别是嵌套反谐振无节点光纤（NANF） ，凭借其独特的
空气导光机制，实现了比传统 SMF 更低的非线性、更低的色度色散以及更低的时延 [3,4]。 
近年来，基于 NANF 的大容量传输研究取得了显著进展。例如，Hong 等人演示了覆
盖超宽带的 NANF 传输[5]；Nespola 等人实现了 61 信道的波分复用（WDM）传输[6]。近
期，Wang 等人更是进一步挖掘了 NANF 在高阶调制传输中的潜力，分别在 2 km NANF 上
实现了概率整形（PS）64QAM [7]以及 PS-256QAM [8]的高速传输。然而，现有的 NANF

--- Page 3 ---
CJL
高速传输实验大多依赖于发射端的高采样率、高分辨率数模转换器（ DAC）来生成高阶调
制信号。高精度 DAC 及其配套的高线性度驱动电路不仅成本高昂，且功耗巨大，这已成
为制约 6G 移动前传大规模部署的关键瓶颈之一。尽管相干检测技术凭借其优异的灵敏度
和相位恢复能力在长距离传输中占据主导地位，但在成本敏感的前传场景下，如何显著降
低发射端的硬件复杂度与功耗仍是亟待解决的核心问题。  
与此同时，移动前传架构正面临着从模拟光载无线（A-RoF）向数字光载无线（D-
RoF）的演进[9]。然而，传统的数字接口（如 CPRI）频谱效率较低，且对高速数模转换器
（DAC）的位宽提出了巨大挑战[10]。为了解决这一矛盾，Delta-Sigma 调制（DSM）技术
被引入光通信领域。DSM 利用过采样和噪声整形原理，能够通过 1-bit 或几比特的低位量
化器实现极高的信噪比（SNR） ，从而在简化发射端硬件的同时显著提升频谱效率[11-13]。杨
雄伟等人此前在 O 波段直接检测系统中，利用 1-bit DSM 成功演示 100 GBaud 信号的传
输，证明了 DSM 在低成本高速链路中的可行性[14]。在追求更高调制阶数方面，Liu 等人结
合神经网络均衡技术，在 W 波段无线系统中实现了 8192-QAM 信号的传输，验证了 
DSM 在超高阶调制中的潜力[15]。在传输距离与可靠性方面，Zhang 等人将 DSM 应用于
电力通信专网，实现了 1024-QAM 信号在 160 km 标准单模光纤中的无中继高可靠传输
[16]。此外，其他研究也展示了 DSM 对 4096QAM 甚至更高阶信号的支持能力[17,18]。 
然而，将 DSM 技术应用于 16384-QAM 等超高阶调制时，仍面临着“器件层”与“物
理层”的双重制约。首先，传统 SMF 的色散效应会导致宽带 DSM 信号的高频噪声分量在
时域上展宽并折叠回基带，破坏噪声整形轮廓；其次，对于 16384-QAM 这种对信噪比要
求极高的格式，简单的 1-bit DSM 量化所能提供的最大信噪比（约 48 dB）已捉襟见肘，成
为系统的短板。若试图通过提高入纤功率来弥补信噪比，又会受限于实芯光纤的非线性阈
值。 
为了解决上述问题，本文提出了一种基于 Delta-Sigma 调制的 16384 QAM 信号 NANF
传输系统。我们利用 NANF 近零色散与低非线性的物理优势作为透明传输通道，配合 2-bit 
DSM 量化技术突破信噪比瓶颈，成功实现了 105 GBaud DSM-PAM4 信号的高保真传输，
并从中重构出高质量的 16384-QAM 信号。该方案在极低 DAC 复杂度下实现了光传输容量
的巨大提升，为未来 6G 移动前传提供了一种兼顾高性能与低成本的新 方案。 
2 基本原理 
2.1  Delta-Sigma 调制原理 
在超高阶 QAM 信号的光传输系统中，为了克服高通过采样率下的 DAC 量化噪声限制
并降低硬件复杂度，Delta-Sigma 调制（DSM）技术被广泛采用。DSM 通过结合过采样
（Oversampling）和噪声整形（Noise Shaping）技术，能够在使用低位数（如 1-bit 或 2-
bit）量化的情况下获得极高的带内信噪比（SNR） 。其基本工作原理如图1 所示。

sources.abstract: Objective Driven by the rapid evolution of wireless communications, emerging applications such as the Internet of Everything, smart cities, and remote control are demanding unprecedented network capabilities. Therefore, the next-generation mobile system must deliver ultra-high capacity, energy-efficient, low-power operation, and massive connectivity. These requirements magnify the scarcity of spectrum resources and the need for ever-higher data rates. Human appetite for capacity is growing exponentially, while the bandwidth currently available is far from sufficient for future data transport. Against this backdrop, the consensus in industry is that the commercial deployment of sixth-generation (6G) mobile networks will begin around 2030. Terahertz (THz) communication, with its abundant spectrum resources, high transmission rates, and high security, has a broad application prospect. Consequently, it is widely recognised by academia and industry worldwide as a key enabler and a prime frequency candidate for 6G. Furthermore, due to the high frequency of optical waves and the large bandwidth of optical devices, photonics-aided THz communication technology has the advantages of flexible frequency tunability, large bandwidth, high spectral efficiency, and low phase noise. It is expected to be combined with fiber communication network to become an effective solution for the next-generation ultra-high-speed wireless broadband access networks. In photonics-aided THz communication systems, the terahertz wave is generated by heterodyning two lasers of different frequencies. The laser linewidth and the weak phase noise of the electrical local oscillator both cause the phase noise of systems. Phase noise degrades signal quality and ultimately limits overall system performance.This study aims to solve the phase noise in photonics-aided THz system by using a radio frequency (RF) pilot-aided method and V-V phase noise estimation algorithms. Methods Firstly, we establish a photonics-aided THz communication system with QPSK modulation in MATLAB R2023b. The simulation initially verifies the effectiveness of a radio-frequency (RF) pilot-aided algorithm in compensating for phase noise. Subsequently, we investigate the performance and influencing factors of phase compensation technology in photonic THz systems by varying parameters such as laser linewidth, pilot power, and signal modulation format. Second, to accurately emulate the phase noise induced by laser linewidth, we conduct simulations in commercial software. This simulation confirms that the RF pilot-aided algorithm’s effectiveness under MHz-level laser linewidths. Finally, we build a photonics-aided THz communication system in the laboratory. THz signals via optical beating of 1550 nm and 1552.4 nm lasers (300 GHz beat frequency), transmitting 20 Gbaud QPSK signals over 20 cm. We  --- Page 16 --- AOS successfully solve the phase noise in photonics-aided THz system by using a radio frequency (RF) pilot-aided method and V-V phase noise estimation algorithms Results and Discussions In MATLAB and commercial software simulations, the RF pilot-aided method effectively compensates for the phase noise (Fig.2, Fig.8). BER increases with laser linewidth (Fig. 7) and first decreases then increases with the pilot-to-signal power ratio (PSR) , reaching its minimum at a PSR of 20 dB (Fig. 4). We also set up a photonics-aided THz wireless transmission system to investigate the impact of pilot-aided algorithm on system performance. Experimentally, received optical power (ROP) from 9 dBm to 12 dBm kept the BER below the 7 % FEC threshold of 0.0038. At 8 dBm, the BER obtained with the V-V phase noise estimation algorithm was 0.006574, slightly above the threshold. Fig. 11 shows that BER and SNR are simultaneously optimized at 11 dBm ROP, where the lowest BER is 0.000046 and the highest SNR is 14.65 dB.At low ROP, the signal is weak and noise becomes relatively prominent, severely compromising the quality of data transmission. Increasing the optical power enhances the signal strength, thereby reducing the bit error rate and improving the signal-to-noise ratio. However, once the power exceeds the linear range of the amplifier, the device saturates, its gain decreases, which causes an increase in the bit error rate, and a reduction in the signal-to-noise ratio.

基于 DFB 激光器产生太赫兹信号的无线传输系统中
的相位补偿技术
李静漪 1,2，杨雄伟 1,2，龙健宇 1,2，余建军 1,2*
1 复旦大学集成芯片与系统全国重点实验室，上海 200433
2 复旦大学未来信息创新学院，上海 200433
摘 要 太赫兹通信具有频谱资源充沛、传输速率高、安全性强等优点，具有广泛的应用前景。
进一步，由于光波的高频率和光学器件的大带宽，光子学辅助的太赫兹通信技术具有信号频
率可调、带宽大、频带利用率高、相位噪声低等优点，有望与光纤通信网络相结合，成为下
一代超高无线带宽接入网络的有效方案。基于此，本论文主要研究光子学辅助的太赫兹通信
系统架构和使用射频导频辅助补偿系统相位噪声的方法。
本文通过采用射频导频辅助和传统 V-V 相位噪声估计算法相结合，成功解决光子辅助太赫
兹系统中的相位噪声问题。首先在 MATLAB 和商用软件中进行模拟仿真，通过均衡、频偏
估计和相位恢复算法对接收端信号进行优化，验证射频导频辅助相位恢复算法的有效性。然
后，搭建光子学辅助太赫兹通信实验平台验证算法性能，实验中，我们成功使用 MHz 级线
宽的分布式反馈激光器(DFB)，实现了 20GBaud QPSK 信号在 300GHz 太赫兹波段的无线传
输，并对比加入射频导频相位恢复算法与否对系统通信性能的影响，对太赫兹通信系统接收
端相位恢复的研究和应用具有一定的实践意义和参考价值。
关键词 光子学辅助技术；太赫兹通信系统；相位恢复；射频导频辅助
中图分类号 O436 文献标志码 A
1 引 言
随着无线通信技术的快速更新和蓬勃发展，万物互联、智慧城市、远程控制等新型应用
出现，下一代移动通信网络将面向超高通信容量、高效能低功耗以及超大规模接入的应用场
景与需求，其频谱资源短缺的问题以及对高速通信的需求日益显现。人们对通信容量的需求
呈指数型增长，目前可用的通信带宽远远无法满足未来数据传输的需求。在这样的背景下，
业内提出 2030 年左右将会正式进入第 6 代移动通信网络商用阶段。太赫兹通信具有频谱资
源充沛、传输速率高、安全性强等优点，被全球学术界与产业界公认为 6G 候选频段与关键
技术的重要组成部分，具有广泛的应用前景。太赫兹波段的频率为 0.1 - 10THz，波长为 0.03
- 3mm，介于微波波段与远红外光波段之间，具有大量未被发掘的领域。太赫兹通信应用场
景广泛，主要包括卫星通信、医学成像、高清视频无损传输等方面。近年来，太赫兹通信受
到全球政府和研究人员的广泛关注，至今已在通信速率和传输性能提升方面取得重大进展。
2004 年，日本 NTT 集团使用单向载流子光电二极管(UTC-PD)在 120GHz 频段达到无线通信

--- Page 3 ---
AOS
系统通信速率 10Gbps，且误码率保持在10−12以下，此系统成功应用于北京奥运会转播[1]。
2008 年，德国 Braunschweig 实验室搭建全电子太赫兹通信系统，在 300GHz 信道上实时传
输模拟视频信号[2]。2011 年，美国贝尔实验室利用全电子器件在 625GHz 实现了通信速率
2.5Gbps 实 验室 距离 的无 误码 传输 [3]。2013 年 ，欧 洲卡 尔斯 鲁厄 理工 学院 搭建 工 作在
237.5GHz 的单输入单输出无线通信系统，将太赫兹光子学与电子学相结合，实现在 20m 距
离上 100Gbit/s 的数据传输速率[4]。2016 年，中国电子科技大学搭建了一个基于肖特基二极
管收发器的 220GHz 全固态太赫兹通信系统，可以在户外环境 200m 的传输距离下实现
3.52Gbps 的 3D 高清视频传输，误码率为 1.92 × 10−6[5]。2023 年，复旦大学余建军课题组
成功实现 256QAM 信号在 335GHz 频段下数据速率 124.8Gbit/s 的传输，通过改进信号处理
技术和增加放大器，将无线传输距离拓展到超过 2km[6]。2025 年，该课题组应用采用数字插
值手段来抵消采样频率偏移的补偿技术，成功在 2×2MIMO 双极化系统中实现了在 322GHz
的太赫兹波段符号速率 60GBaud 的 64QAM 信号通信距离为有线 20km 和无线 3m 的传输[7]。
太赫兹通信系统可按照其产生太赫兹信号的方式分为 3 类：纯电子学方法，光子学辅助
法和量子级联法。纯电子学的太赫兹通信系统通过低频微波的倍频生成太赫兹信号，具有体
积小、易集成、功耗低等优点，支持大功率太赫兹信号辐射，有利于实现太赫兹信号的长距
离传输。但囿于电子器件工作带宽、调制效率等因素，系统带宽受到限制。与基于电子学的
太赫兹技术相比，光子学太赫兹通信技术由于光波频率高、光学器件带宽大，具有信号频率
可调、带宽大、频带利用率高、相位噪声低等优点，可以与光纤通信网络相结合，实现超高
速、超宽带无线通信。
光子学辅助方案中，太赫兹信号通过两个频率不同的激光器外差拍频产生，激光器线宽
和本地振荡器电信号的微弱初始噪声都会导致传输系统中的相位噪声。过大的相位噪声会造
成信号质量下降，通信信噪比降低，误码率增大，影响系统性能。对于相位噪声的处理，一
种方法是调整硬件参数，增加辅助电路，文献[8]提出在电路中加入延迟环，对比当前信号
和延迟信号相位得到并消除相位噪声。文献[9]引入全光下变频技术，以避免电混频器引入
的相位噪声。还可以使用受激布里渊散射激光器的光子发射器和接收器搭建电路[10]。这样
的方式虽然性能较优，但电路结构相对复杂，调试难度大，会增加光子辅助太赫兹通信系统
的成本。另一种方式是在接收端数字基带结合信号处理算法对相位噪声进行补偿，基于经典
的 Viterbi-Viterbi 算法通过对信号进行 M 次方操作以移除相位调制信息，估算并补偿相位噪
声[11]。为提高高阶 QAM 对激光线宽的容忍度，研究人员陆续提出了盲相位搜索算法(BPS)[12]，
改进盲相位搜索算法(BPS/ML)[13]以及 QPSK 分割与最大似然相结合的新型分割算法[14]用于

--- Page 4 ---
AOS
高阶 QAM 调制信号的载波频率和相位估计等方法。文献[15]提出采用自校正的方法对相位
噪声进行迭代补偿。这种方式成本更低，更容易结合各种信号处理算法，方便调试和测试。
文献[16]提出了提出并仿真验证了一种基于端到端深度学习的相位噪声补偿方案,通过学习
信道中相位噪声与色散的相互作用，进行星座成形与 BPS 训练，提升系统对相位噪声的补
偿能力。同时，也有一些学者创新性提出了硬件和算法相结合的相位噪声补偿方式，文献[17]
提出一种使用注入锁定激光器消除系统频偏，再通过比例-积分-微分算法将相位差转换为幅
度变化，使用微处理器进行运算，驱动压电陶瓷拉伸光纤以补偿光相位的方式。
本项目重点研究使用接收端数字信号处理补偿相位噪声的方法，提出一种通过射频导频
辅助补偿光子学辅助太赫兹通信系统中的相位噪声的方法。借助射频导频辅助算法，可以有
效地解决太赫兹通信系统中的相位噪声问题，降低系统误码率，提升信噪比，并提升光子学
辅助太赫兹系统的激光线宽容限，同时算法难度低，运算量小，可以节省计算时间，减少成
本。采用具有 MHz 级激光器线宽的分布反馈式激光器(DFB)搭建光子学辅助的太赫兹通信
系统传输 QPSK 信号，使用射频导频辅助法与经典的 V-V 前馈载波相位恢复算法相结合补
偿相位噪声，降低系统信号传输误码率，提升系统性能。
2 仿真实验
2.1 光子学辅助太赫兹通信系统 MATLAB 仿真
利用 MATLAB R2023b 建立一个调制格式为 QPSK 的光子学辅助太赫兹通信系统，如
图 1 所示。发送端 DSP 中，生成符号速率为 20GBaud 的 QPSK 信号。QPSK 信号的调制分
为 I、Q 两路，使用相位差 90 度互相正交的两个 300GHz 载波进行调制。具体流程为：随机
产生 0、1 码元作为原始数据序列，进行双极性转换将 0、1 序列转换为±1，再通过串并转
换得到并行的两路 IQ 信号，两路信号各传输 1bit 码元，序列中每 2 个 bit 映射为一个符号，
本项目将 QPSK 信号{11,01,00,10}对应映射到{/4,3/4,5/4,7/4}上。调制完成的 QPSK
信号经过 2 倍上采样后，使用滚降因子为 0.01 的升余弦滤波器进行脉冲整形。插入射频导
频信号，再对 I、Q 两路信号进行载波调制。

sources.abstract: Objective 　 The rapid advancement of data - intensive technologies ,  including the Internet of Things ( IoT )  and cloud  computing ,  presents unprecedented capacity challenges for current optical networks .  Traditional optical communication  networks ,  primarily utilizing silica single - mode fiber ( SMF ),  have historically achieved exponential growth in information  transmission capacity .  However ,  the Kerr nonlinearity inherent in single - mode fibers imposes limitations ,  with maximum  throughput approaching the theoretical limit of approximately 100  Tbit/s ,  insufficient for future data traffic demands .  In  response ,  space - division multiplexing ( SDM )  technology has emerged as a crucial solution to overcome the capacity  constraints of single - mode fibers by enabling multiple independent data channels within a single wavelength through  parallel spatial channels ,  offering a revolutionary approach to expanding optical network capacity . Methods 　 This research presents a high - capacity ,  long - haul multidimensional multiplexing transmission system utilizing  weakly coupled multi - core few - mode fiber ( MC - FMF ),  achieving efficient transmission of 48  Gbaud broadband signals  through a weakly coupled 19 - core 6 - mode fiber .  The system combines SDM technology with wavelength - division  multiplexing ( WDM )  and polarization - division multiplexing ( PDM )  technologies .  This implementation facilitates  independent transmission of four spatial modes ( LP 11 a ,  LP 11 b ,  LP 21 a ,  and LP 21 b )  and their corresponding polarization across  80  wavelength channels . sources.abstract: Objective 　 The explosive growth of data - intensive services in the Beyond 5 G ( B 5 G )  and 6 G era ,  such as ultra - high - definition video  streaming ,  virtual/augmented reality ,  and internet of things applications ,  has imposed stringent requirements on access networks ,   including high capacity ,  low latency ,  and lightweight deployment .  To support these demands ,  radio - over - fiber ( RoF )  systems  combined with multiple - input multiple - output ( MIMO )  and polarization - division multiplexing ( PDM )  technologies are considered  highly promising .  However ,  conventional MIMO - RoF architectures depend on multiple single - mode fibers for parallel transmission ,   resulting in excessive fiber resource consumption ,  which hinders the large - scale deployment of compact base stations .  Therefore ,  it is  necessary to develop a novel RoF architecture that not only minimizes fiber resources but also enhances integration ,  maintains high  performance ,  and facilitates miniaturized base station deployment . Methods 　 To address these issues ,  a structure - simplified 2 × 2  PDM - MIMO RoF system is proposed .  It utilizes a multi - core hollow - core fiber ( HCF )  to simultaneously transmit two orthogonal polarization signals within a single fiber .  This work highlights the  feasibility and effectiveness of combining multi - core multiplexing with polarization - division MIMO transmission ,  demonstrating an  important step toward future cost - effective and scalable RoF micro - base station deployment .  The schematic and experimental setup  are shown in Figure 1  and Figure 2 ,  respectively .  The modulated signal is separated into two orthogonal polarization channels by a  polarization beam splitter ( PBS ).  Meanwhile ,  another laser is used as the local oscillator ( LO ).  The LO is also divided by a

摘 要  为满足 B 5 G/ 6 G 时代对高容量 、 低时延和可集成无线接入系统的需求 ， 多输入多输出 （ MIMO ） 光纤无线融合

系统架构 。 该方案利用多芯空芯光纤实现两路正交偏振信号在单根光纤内同步传输 ， 结合天线极化复用与多输入多

sources.abstract: Significance 　 The evolution of sixth - generation ( 6 G )  mobile communication systems necessitates unprecedented demands  for ultra - high data rates ,  ultra - low latency ,  and ultra - reliable connectivity across terrestrial ,  aerial ,  maritime ,  and  extraterrestrial environments .  While traditional radio frequency ( RF )  communication has served as a foundation ,  it  encounters significant limitations including spectrum scarcity ,  interference ,  and high energy consumption .  These  constraints particularly affect emerging applications such as holographic communication ,  real - time industrial automation ,   autonomous vehicles ,  and massive - scale Internet of Things ( IoT ),  where current technologies struggle to meet stringent  performance requirements . Visib le Light Communication ( VLC ),  operating in the 380 ‒ 700  n m wavelength range of the electrom agnetic  spectrum ,  presents a viable solution .  The visible spectrum provides an extensive ,  unlicensed bandwidth of approximately  400  THz ,  enabling high - capacity communication without increasing spectral congestion .  Furthermore ,  VLC integration  with existing lighting infrastructure through LEDs and laser diodes ( LDs )  facilitates cost - effective deployment while  --- Page 18 --- 1306003 - 18 亮点文章 · 特邀综述 第  45  卷  第  13  期 / 2025  年  7  月 / 光学学报 serving dual purposes of illumination and communication .  This approach enhances energy efficiency and supports  sustainable communication technologies . VLC demonstrates distinct advantages in specific environments .  Its resistance to electromagnetic interference makes it  particularly suitable for hospitals ,  airplanes ,  and factories ,  while its line - of - sight characteristics ensure robust physical - layer security — crucial for defense ,  financial ,  and industrial applications .  Additionally ,  VLC proves effective for  underwater communication where RF signals face severe attenuation ,  and in dense indoor environments where RF  performance deteriorates .  The high directionality and limited signal dispersion of visible light enable efficient spatial reuse ,   improving system capacity in multi - user scenarios . The academic and industrial communities worldwide have identified VLC as a crucial component of 6 G development .   Various governmental and research initiatives actively promote VLC standardization and commercialization . user_papers.title: 抗色散双偏振双二进制直接检测PON系统研究
user_papers.institution: 上海大学特种光纤与光接入网重点实验室
user_papers.abstract: 本文提出了一种双偏振双二进制系统，能够缓解直接检测PON中的码间串扰与非线性失真。通过将复杂度转移至发射端，本方案实现了算法资源需求较低的简单直接检测接收机，并确保与现有ONU的兼容性。与PAM4相比，DP-ODB格式在高SNR下表现相当，但在低SNR场景下，尤其是高累积色散条件下对码间串扰鲁棒性显著更强。实验验证了在42.5 ps/nm累积色散处达到-23 dBm的接收灵敏度，在170 ps/nm处达到-19 dBm。此外，本文测试了对SBS的容忍度，最后得出当发射功率超过10 dBm时，分别实现了33 dB和29 dB的功率预算，证实了该方案在PON系统中的可行性。
core_contributions: 提出双偏振双二进制（DP-ODB）传输架构
实现40 GBaud信号传输，扩展至50 GBaud验证
在42.5 ps/nm累积色散下达到-23 dBm接收灵敏度
在170 ps/nm累积色散下达到-19 dBm接收灵敏度
SBS容忍度从7 dBm提升至16 dBm（提升9 dB）
实现33 dB和29 dB的功率预算
接收机复杂度显著降低（无需Volterra均衡器）
technical_innovation: 双偏振架构：功率分配2:1，避免相干接收机复杂度
ODB调制：抑制光载波，提高SBS阈值
预编码与π相位交替：实现符号间干扰的相消作用
直接检测：平方律检测实现四电平强度信号
